title: Obione cuneata
taxonomy: Obione cuneata is classified within the family Amaranthaceae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants in the same family. More specifically, it belongs to the subfamily Salsoloideae within Amaranthaceae. The classification also places it in the order Caryophyllales and the class Magnoliopsida. Further classification details can be found in the taxonomic hierarchy of plant species.
uses: Obione cuneata has been traditionally used in some parts of the world for its medicinal properties, although its effectiveness has not been extensively scientifically proven. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In some folk remedies, it is used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions and digestive problems. The plant's medicinal uses are largely based on its supposed ability to reduce inflammation and fight infections. However, more research is needed to confirm its actual medicinal benefits and safety for human consumption.
